title: bug(quadlet): implicit network dependencies timeout
body:
### Issue Description

When starting a Container Quadlet using `systemctl` command, nothing is visible through the standard output for 2 minutes, before the container starts.

Thanks to @l0rd we were able to narrow down the problem. It is related to the `podman-user-wait-network-online.service`, which is implicitly added by the Quadlet systemd generator.

According to the documentation[^1], adding the section bellow fixes the problem

[^1]: https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html#implicit-network-dependencies

```ini
[Quadlet]
DefaultDependencies=false
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue on Linux with QEMU

1. `podman machine init --now qemu-demo`
2. write `nginx.container` container quadlet as user (
```ini
[Container]
PublishPort=8888:80
Image=docker.io/library/nginx:latest
```
3. Inside the machine `systemctl daemon-reload`
4. Inside the machine `systemctl --user start nginx`
5. assert nothing is happening for 2 minutes

### Describe the results you received

When running the `systemctl --user start nginx` command, we need to wait around 2 minutes before the finally the container is started. 

Here are the journalctl logs from a little before running the systemctl start command, until the container starts.

```
$: journalctl -f
May 14 17:16:42 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: Started podman-13318.scope.
May 14 17:16:42 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: Starting podman-user-wait-network-online.service - Wait for system level network-online.target as user....
May 14 17:16:42 localhost.localdomain sh[13352]: inactive
May 14 17:16:43 localhost.localdomain sh[13354]: inactive
May 14 17:16:43 localhost.localdomain sh[13356]: inactive
May 14 17:16:44 localhost.localdomain sh[13358]: inactive
May 14 17:16:44 localhost.localdomain sh[13360]: inactive
[...]
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain sh[13922]: inactive
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: podman-user-wait-network-online.service: start operation timed out. Terminating.
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: podman-user-wait-network-online.service: Main process exited, code=killed, status=15/TERM
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: podman-user-wait-network-online.service: Failed with result 'timeout'.
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: Failed to start podman-user-wait-network-online.service - Wait for system level network-online.target as user..
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: podman-user-wait-network-online.service: Consumed 1.808s CPU time, 2.2M memory peak.
May 14 17:18:12 localhost.localdomain systemd[1310]: Starting nginx-exposed.service...
```

### Describe the results you expected

The nginx container should start directly

### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Do not reproduce on WSL2 windows, nor on Podman Native

Issue encountered on linux QEMU, Apple HV, Apple Libkrun

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: That sounds like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24796. If the scope for this here is just podman-machine we can make sure network-online.target is being run as root so there should be no timeout.

> That sounds like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24796.

@Luap99 I was reading the issue, and try a few things to narrow down the problem, something odd is that `network-online.target` is dead and always be when I start a fedora base machine.

```shell
$: podman machine start
$: podman machine ssh systemctl status network-online.target
○ network-online.target - Network is Online
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/network-online.target; static)
     Active: inactive (dead)
       Docs: man:systemd.special(7)
             https://systemd.io/NETWORK_ONLINE
```

which is clearly the root of the issue here, as the `podman-user-wait-network-online.service` check for this service to be active.

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/6d23f2275071ebea641ce35762f669967da976b3/contrib/systemd/user/podman-user-wait-network-online.service#L11

---

### `network-online.service`

I am not  quite sure to understand why the `network-online.service` do not get active, I don't have enough understanding to find the root cause for now.

 I was reading https://systemd.io/NETWORK_ONLINE/ and with the following

> `network-online.target` is a target that actively waits until the network is “up”, where the definition of “up” is defined by the network management software. Usually it indicates a configured, routable IP address of some kind. Its primary purpose is to actively delay activation of services until the network has been set up.

I understand that "something" inside the machine is suppose to make it available and up and active. But what?

Looking at the `NetworkManager-wait-online.service` we can see the following status and journal

```
$: systemctl status NetworkManager-wait-online.service
○ NetworkManager-wait-online.service - Network Manager Wait Online
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/NetworkManager-wait-online.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
    Drop-In: /usr/lib/systemd/system/service.d
             └─10-timeout-abort.conf, 50-keep-warm.conf
     Active: inactive (dead)
       Docs: man:NetworkManager-wait-online.service(8)
```

```
$: journalctl --unit NetworkManager-wait-online.service
-- Boot 853e44b5b8454f4590242f0806c3b83c --
May 21 14:30:20 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Starting NetworkManager-wait-online.service - Network Manager Wait Online...
May 21 14:30:20 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Finished NetworkManager-wait-online.service - Network Manager Wait Online.
May 21 14:32:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: NetworkManager-wait-online.service: Deactivated successfully.
May 21 14:32:16 localhost.localdomain systemd[1]: Stopped NetworkManager-wait-online.service - Network Manager Wait Online.
```

Opening it, we can see it execute the following

```
ExecStart=/usr/bin/nm-online -s -q
```

but running the command myself got a zero exit code, restarting the service makes it active up and running, so my other guess is that something _Deactivated_ the `NetworkManager-wait-online.service`, but what? who?

something must pull in the network-online.target, since by default no unit does that is never is activated

What is needed is something like this to "enable" it: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/24796#issuecomment-2527813352

We should certainly just do that in machine so rootless quadlets work fine.
